title: SoftBank subsidiary working with Intel to develop radical new ZAM memory is now receiving Japanese gov't subsidies new memory designed as a lower-power HBM for AI workloads
summary: SaiMemory, a SoftBank subsidiary, secures funding to develop ZAM memory, a next-gen DRAM architecture designed to address limitations of High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM) for AI workloads, requiring lower power consumption.
insight: Lower-power memory crucial for AI workloads to boost efficiency.

title: Intel CEO says he can 'think of no better partner than Elon Musk' to explore 'unconventional' ways to improve chip manufacturing TeraFab partnership aims to rethink how chips are made to reduce costs
summary: Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan praises TeraFab partnership with Elon Musk, aiming to rethink chip manufacturing. The partnership aims to reduce costs by exploring unconventional ways to make chips. Details about the relationship are still limited.
insight: Revolutionizing chip manufacturing through unconventional, cost-reducing approaches emerges.

title: First Intel Wildcat Lake laptop spotted in the wild, geared to compete with MacBook Neo features an aluminum chassis with 11W fanless mode
summary: Intel's Wildcat Lake laptop has been spotted, featuring an aluminum chassis and fanless mode. The laptop, an Intel reference machine, showcases the new CPU's capabilities. It also highlights the CPU's power limits and modes.
insight: Intel's Wildcat Lake CPU offers power efficiency and flexibility.

title: Intel shutters open-source evangelism program and archives key community projects closures point to significant shift in open-source leadership
summary: Intel has shut down its open-source evangelism program and archived key community projects, highlighting a significant shift in its open-source leadership. This move is part of a broader pullback in Intel's open-source footprint amid ongoing restructuring. The closures on GitHub underscore this trend.
insight: Intel's strategic shift may reduce open-source innovation and collaboration

title: Crypto scam takes advantage of Strait of Hormuz crisis by taking fake payments, leading to two ships being fired upon two vessels reportedly fell victim and paid fake 'Iranian authorities'
summary: Two ships in the Strait of Hormuz were scammed into paying fake transit fees in crypto, reportedly falling victim to impostors claiming to be Iranian authorities.
insight: Crypto scams exploit global crises for financial gain.

title: Anthropic surpasses biggest rival OpenAI in secondary market valuation surges to $1 trillion amid frantic investor interest
summary: Anthropic's secondary market valuation has surged to $1 trillion, surpassing OpenAI's $880 billion, driven by intense investor demand for AI exposure.
insight: Speculative pricing in secondary markets fuels AI valuation boom.

title: SpaceX says it is going to begin manufacturing GPUs $1.75 trillion IPO listing reportedly includes in-house GPU production
summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX set to produce 'own GPUs' at its own multi-billion fab as the company warns that it may be unable to purchase all the silicon it needs to meet its goals.

title: UK spy agency releases malware-blocking gadget for HDMI and DisplayPort cables SilentGlass blocks malicious traffic traveling between display and computer
summary: The UK's NCSC has released SilentGlass, a malware-blocking gadget for HDMI and DisplayPort cables. It protects government estates and is now available through Goldilock Labs. This device blocks malicious traffic between displays and computers.
insight: Enhanced cybersecurity measures for public and private networks

title: Hands On: Corsair s Frame Configurator brings dozens of customization options (and rippling wood) to its 4000 Series PC cases
summary: Corsair's 4000-Series cases offer customization options such as unique wood panels and RGB lighting. The configurator allows users to select from various components, including three motherboard trays. This range of choices enhances the personalization of PC builds.
insight: Highly customizable PC cases cater to individual user preferences

title: You can now run Linux on your ancient Windows 95 desktop with a new tool very old Windows PCs, back to Intel 486, can cooperatively run very modern Linux kernels with WSL9x
summary: Users with ancient Windows PCs can now run modern Linux kernels with WSL9x.
insight: Reviving legacy hardware with modern Linux capabilities

title: More than 30,000 Samsung union members take to the streets to demand an average bonus of $400,000 per worker May 21 strike date looms, union points to rival SK hynix granting higher bonuses to its employees
summary: Samsung union members stage a rally demanding higher pay and bonuses, citing SK hynix's higher compensation. The union is pushing for an average bonus of $400,000 per worker. A strike is looming on May 21.
insight: Labor unrest threatens Samsung's operations due to pay disparity claims.

title: U.S. Commerce Secretary says Nvidia still hasn't sold any H200 AI GPUs to China Chinese government is blocking imports in an attempt to push domestic semiconductor industry
summary: Nvidia's H200 AI GPU sales to China remain stalled, despite ban lift. The Chinese government is hindering imports to boost domestic semiconductors. Nvidia chips are still not reaching Chinese companies.
insight: China's semiconductor ambitions hindered by import restrictions on Nvidia chips

title: Bolt Graphics tapes out its first Zeus GPU test chip on TSMC 12nm firm touts 17x lower cost of compute
summary: Bolt Graphics has successfully taped out its first Zeus GPU test chip on TSMC's 12nm process, marking a significant milestone in the company's transition from FPGA emulation to manufactured silicon. This achievement demonstrates Bolt's progress in developing a competitive GPU solution. The Zeus GPU is touted to offer 17x lower cost of compute.
insight: Competitive GPU solution arrives with massive cost savings potential

title: Wavkong V2700 Wi-Fi 6 router offers long-range and greater coverage with radio processing unit (RPU) for cleaner signals (Crowdfunding)
summary: The Wavkong V2700 Wi-Fi 6 router offers 15,000 sq ft coverage with its Radio Processing Unit (RPU) for cleaner signals. It features a MediaTek Filogic 820 processor, 256 MB RAM, and 4x Gigabit Ethernet ports. The router is available through crowdfunding.
insight: Enhanced WiFi coverage without the need for mesh networks

title: Argon Industria HMI 5C An industrial aluminum enclosure for the 5-inch Raspberry Pi Touch Display 2
summary: The Argon Industria HMI 5C is an industrial-grade aluminum enclosure for the Raspberry Pi 5 and official 5-inch Raspberry Pi Touch Display 2. It provides a compact Human Machine Interface (HMI) system with VESA and panel mounting capabilities, and allows access to various ports via an internal IO board. This enclosure is suitable for control panels, automation interfaces, and embedded display applications.
insight: Enables industrial-grade Raspberry Pi solutions for control panel applications.

title: Ubuntu 26.04 LTS Resolute Raccoon released with Linux 7.0
summary: Ubuntu 26.04 LTS Resolute Raccoon, with Linux 7.0 kernel, has been released with improved features and security updates.
insight: Latest Linux kernel supports Intel's newer Core Ultra Series processors.

title: Microchip PIC16F132 and PIC18-Q35 low-power 8-bit MCUs feature CPLD-like Configurable Logic Blocks (CLB)
summary: Microchip introduces the PIC16F132 and PIC18-Q35 MCUs with integrated Configurable Logic Blocks (CLB), reducing CPU load and power consumption. The CLB architecture executes logic functions on dedicated hardware for deterministic behavior in applications like motor control and industrial automation. The PIC18-Q35 offers higher logic density than the PIC16F132x.
insight: Hardware-based logic execution reduces CPU load and power consumption.

title: AAEON BOXER-6407-TWL A slim Twin Lake embedded PC for semiconductor manufacturing and digital signage
summary: The AAEON BOXER-6407-TWL is a slim, fanless embedded PC suitable for semiconductor manufacturing and digital signage applications, featuring Intel Twin Lake processors, up to 32GB DDR5 memory, and various connectivity options.
insight: Optimized for extreme environments and space-constrained installations.

title: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs to bankroll its AI ambitions
summary: Meta is cutting 8,000 jobs to fund its AI investments and partnerships.
insight: AI spending drives layoffs in the tech industry, shifting priorities.

title: Intel showcases Wildcat Lake reference laptop with aluminum chassis and fanless design
summary: Intel has showcased a Wildcat Lake reference laptop with an aluminum chassis and fanless design. It features a six-core CPU in a 2P+4LPE configuration. The SoC includes Xe graphics cores and a dedicated 17 TOPS NPU.
insight: Intel's Wildcat Lake aims for improved performance and power efficiency.

title: Linux may drop legacy network drivers amid surge in AI-generated bug reports
summary: Linux may drop legacy network drivers due to AI-generated bug reports. The open-source community is concerned about the impact of generative AI services on software development and bug reporting. This could lead to a significant change in the way Linux handles its extensive collection of legacy device drivers.
insight: AI-generated bug reports threaten the future of legacy code maintenance

title: Anker is betting its new chip can change how AI runs in earbuds
summary: Anker's new chip uses compute-in-memory architecture for efficient AI processing in earbuds. This reduces the need for data transfer between memory and compute units. The result is faster and more energy-efficient AI operations.
insight: Revolutionizes AI processing by reducing energy consumption and latency.

title: An AI agent just designed a complete RISC-V CPU from scratch in 12 hours
summary: VerCore, a complete RISC-V CPU, was designed by AI in 12 hours, featuring a 5-stage pipeline design and 1.48GHz clock speed.
insight: AI can revolutionize chip design with unprecedented speed and efficiency.

title: Duke Nukem 3D gets path tracing and DLSS through a new fan-made mod
summary: A fan-made mod, Duke-RT, brings path tracing and DLSS to Duke Nukem 3D using an open-source code that incorporates ray-tracing rendering and a fork of the original Build engine, Raze.
insight: Revolutionizing retro gaming with cutting-edge graphics technology integration

title: SpaceX may build its own GPUs to reduce chip supply risks
summary: SpaceX may build its own GPUs to reduce chip supply risks, according to its S-1 filing. This move is part of the company's substantial capital expenditures ahead of its expected IPO this summer.
insight: Reducing reliance on external supply chains for critical components

title: Interactive Listening Museum lets you compare the sounds of 36 mechanical keyboards
summary: The Interactive Listening Museum allows users to compare sounds of 36 mechanical keyboards, spanning from vintage to modern models.
insight: In-depth sound comparison empowers informed keyboard purchasing decisions.

title: Microsoft says Windows 11 changes are "directly influenced" by users
summary: Microsoft admits Windows 11 changes are influenced by user feedback, marking a shift in their approach.
insight: Users' opinions are finally driving product development at Microsoft.

title: Mozilla uses Anthropic's Mythos to uncover 271 bugs in Firefox 150
summary: Mozilla utilized Anthropic's Mythos to identify 271 bugs in Firefox 150, giving defenders an advantage over attackers.
insight: AI-powered bug detection levels the playing field for cybersecurity defenders.

title: Kalshi fines candidates for betting on their own elections, but one says he wanted to get caught
summary: Kalshi fined candidates for insider trading on their own elections, citing newly released safeguards.
insight: New safeguards uncover instances of insider trading among political candidates.

title: Sony AI builds a table tennis robot that reacts faster than humans can see
summary: Sony AI's table tennis robot reacts faster than human vision, marking a breakthrough in real-time physical sports.
insight: Advancements in AI can overcome physical limitations in real-time sports.

title: Microsoft's next Xbox could get Asus and MSI variants, leaker claims
summary: Asus and MSI may release Xbox variants with AMD GPU, leaker claims. Microsoft's next Xbox, codenamed Project Helix, features a semi-custom SoC. The news suggests possible partnerships with PC builders.
insight: Potential partnerships could expand Xbox's reach and accessibility options

title: Tesla says soaring gas prices are driving more EV demand
summary: Tesla reports surge in global EV demand, with US seeing slight growth.
insight: Soaring gas prices drive increased demand for electric vehicles.

title: Google says AI now generates 75% of its new code, up from 25% in 2024
summary: Google reports significant increase in AI-generated code, now at 75%, up from 25% in 2024. This shift is largely due to the internal deployment of Gemini models for code generation and refactoring. The use of AI tools is also being tied to performance reviews beyond engineering.
insight: AI-generated code efficiency transforms Google's development process fundamentally.
